YOUR JOURNEY TO EMOTIONAL RECOVERY
STARTS NOW

Begin Your Journey

Young Adult PHP

Intensive partial hospitalization designed exclusively for adults ages 18–33, five days a week in Marin County.

Trauma & Mental Health

Evidence-based treatment for depression, anxiety, PTSD, bipolar disorder, BPD, and co-occurring conditions.

Emotional Recovery

Rebuilding the relationship with yourself — healing, growth, and durable coping skills for the life you are building.

Mental Health in Emerging Adulthood

When people experience the challenges of early adulthood — identity, relationships, career, separation from family — the effects can be profound and lasting. Many young adults begin to lose touch with parts of themselves they haven't yet had the chance to build. The result is depression, anxiety, disconnection, and a sense that something is fundamentally wrong. These effects are real, and they are treatable.

Why Young Adults Need Different Care

Research consistently shows that early intervention in the 18–33 age window produces significantly better long-term outcomes. What begins as manageable stress can — without support — compound into patterns that are far harder to address later. Treating mental health and developmental challenges together, rather than sequentially, is the most effective path to lasting recovery and emotional freedom.

The Path

"The goal is to understand how the struggles of the present are connected to the past — and to forge a path toward the life you actually want."

We start by creating an environment that is respectful, compassionate, and safe. We avoid pathologizing symptoms but rather promote a strengthening and integration of thoughts, feelings, and behavior.

The therapeutic groups are designed to both increase knowledge and awareness. It is through increased awareness where the real work begins. You will learn so much about your capabilities during this process — it is a place of healing, growth, and expansion. The point at which the path becomes clear and new discoveries begin.

What you will discover:
  • An increased ability to handle difficult situations with ease
  • A solid foundation for emotional healing to continue
  • Freedom from patterns that have kept you stuck
  • The ability to live in the present rather than the past
  • Genuine connection with peers who understand
  • Skills you will carry with you for the rest of your life

If you are a young adult struggling with depression, anxiety, trauma, or emotional pain — or if you are a family member who wants to help someone you love — let us help you find clarity and forge a new path toward freedom.

Begin Your Journey
"The first time in my life that I felt like the people around me actually understood what I was going through. Journey gave me the tools I needed — and the community I didn't know I was missing."
— Journey BHC Graduate, Age 24

About Journey Behavioral Healthcare

Our Mission

To provide young adults ages 18–33 with intensive, developmentally-informed mental health treatment that stabilizes acute symptoms, builds durable coping skills, and equips them to engage fully in the life they are building — delivered with clinical expertise, genuine warmth, and deep respect for the unique challenges of this life stage.

Why Marin County

Marin County has a documented and serious mental health treatment gap. Nearly 20% of adults report mental health challenges — nearly double California's statewide average. In 2024, 37 Marin County residents died by suicide. The county's natural beauty, its wealth, and its cultural sophistication exist alongside a profound scarcity of intensive mental health resources for young adults.

Journey Behavioral Healthcare was founded to fill this gap — to be the program that Marin's young adults and their families, therapists, and physicians can depend on. We are the only dedicated young adult Partial Hospitalization Program serving Marin County and the surrounding Bay Area.

Our Values

Developmentally-Informed

Every clinical decision accounts for where our clients are in the process of becoming who they will be — not just their diagnosis at this moment.

Radical Compassion

Unconditional positive regard. Zero judgment. A genuine belief in every person's capacity to heal, grow, and build a life worth living.

Clinical Excellence

Evidence-based care delivered by California-licensed professionals with specific training and genuine affinity for young adult mental health.

Peer Connection

Shared experience is one of the most powerful therapeutic tools available. Our cohort model turns that into clinical leverage.

Family as Resource

We engage families as collaborative partners while honoring each young adult's emerging autonomy and right to direct their own care.

Continuity of Care

Seamless coordination from first contact through active PHP, discharge, and step-down support. Recovery doesn't end at discharge.

Admissions & Intake

Our Program

A Day at Journey

Monday – Friday  ·  9:00 AM – 3:00 PM  ·  San Rafael, Marin County

9:00 AMMorning Check-In GroupMindfulness-based grounding · mood check · setting intentions for the day
9:30 AMDBT / CBT Skills GroupRotating modules: Emotion Regulation · Distress Tolerance · Interpersonal Effectiveness · Mindfulness
10:45 AMYoung Adult Process GroupPeer therapy focused on identity, life transitions, relationships, and real life challenges
12:00 PMLunch BreakRest · peer connection · optional walk
12:45 PMIndividual AppointmentsIndividual therapy · medication management · case management (rotating by schedule)
2:00 PMSpecialty GroupWeekly rotation: Life Skills · Digital Wellness · Nature-Based Mindfulness · Family Psychoeducation
3:00 PMClosing GroupDaily reflection · progress acknowledgment · intentions for tomorrow
Clinical Services

Group Therapy

DBT skills groups, process therapy, and psychoeducation in a peer community of young adults who understand. Maximum group size of 10 ensures depth and real connection.

Individual Therapy

Two to three sessions per week with your assigned licensed therapist. Evidence-based modalities including CBT, DBT, ACT, trauma-focused therapy, and motivational interviewing.

Psychiatric Care

Medication evaluation, management, and psychoeducation by a board-certified psychiatrist or psychiatric nurse practitioner with young adult expertise.

Case Management

Insurance coordination, academic accommodations, employment, housing resources, and discharge planning. The practical infrastructure of recovery.

Family Therapy

With your consent, we involve family as clinical allies — not to report on you, but to help build the support system your recovery needs. Offered bi-weekly.

Life Skills & Digital Wellness

Executive functioning, financial basics, healthy relationships, career navigation, and a clinical look at how social media and technology affect your mental health.

Nature-Based Therapy

Weekly evidence-based ecotherapy in Marin County's extraordinary landscape. Movement, mindfulness, and the healing power of being outside.

Safety Planning

A personalized, collaborative safety plan built with you — not for you. Updated regularly throughout your time in the program and yours to keep at discharge.

Begin Here

How It Works
01

Reach Out

Call, email, or complete our form. You will hear from our Admissions Coordinator within one business day — often the same day. The first conversation is just that: a conversation.

02

Phone Screening

A brief, confidential call to understand what is going on and confirm that a PHP level of care is appropriate. No pressure, no commitment. Just clarity.

03

Clinical Assessment

A 60–90 minute in-person or telehealth assessment with a licensed clinician. We will learn about you, answer every question, and determine next steps together.

04

Insurance Verification

Our team handles insurance verification and prior authorization on your behalf. We will be clear about what to expect financially before your first day — no surprises.

05

Your First Day

A warm, thoughtfully-structured first day designed to help you settle in, meet the team, and understand what recovery here looks like. We take it at your pace.

Who We Serve

Clinical Criteria

  • Adults ages 18–33
  • A mental health diagnosis causing significant difficulty in daily life
  • Need more intensive treatment than weekly therapy — but not 24-hour inpatient care
  • Able to maintain basic safety with structured PHP support
  • Can attend Monday–Friday, 9 AM–3 PM with reliable transportation
  • Depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, PTSD, BPD, adjustment disorders, co-occurring substance use

Insurance We Work With

Anthem Blue Cross CA Blue Shield CA Aetna Cigna UnitedHealthcare Medi-Cal Medicare Private Pay

ACA dependent coverage accepted for adults under 26 on a parent's plan. Sliding scale available for documented financial need.

For Families

Privacy Comes First

All clients are adults. We cannot share any information about your family member's treatment without their written consent. This is the law — and it is also the right way to support recovery.

You Can Still Reach Out

You can call us with questions about the program itself — what PHP is, how admissions works, what family involvement looks like — without us confirming anything about any specific person.

Family Therapy Is Available

With the client's consent, we actively involve families as collaborative partners. Family therapy is offered bi-weekly; family psychoeducation groups are available throughout treatment.

The First Step Is the Hardest

If your family member is resistant to seeking help, you are not alone. Our admissions team can talk with you about how to approach that conversation — and what your realistic options are.
